response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh was N. Chandrababu Naidu. He was the leader of the Telugu Desam Party. His tenure began in 1995 and continued until 2014.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu was re-elected in 2014. He formed the government with the support of the YSR Congress Party. His leadership was marked by economic development and infrastructure projects.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu resigned in 2014 after the party split. He later formed the Telugu Desam Party again. His political career continued with new alliances and strategies.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu's government focused on education and healthcare reforms. He initiated several welfare schemes for the people. His policies aimed at improving the quality of life.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu faced criticism for corruption allegations. His party was accused of misusing public funds. However, he maintained his political influence.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu's leadership was characterized by strong governance. He emphasized economic growth and employment opportunities. His vision shaped the state's development agenda.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu's political journey continued despite challenges. He remained a prominent figure in Andhra Pradesh politics. His legacy is reflected in the state's progress.  

***  
N. Chandrababu Naidu's leadership had a lasting impact on the state. He played a key role in shaping Andhra Pradesh's future. His contributions are still recognized today.
